title: Disaggregation Architecture + Extent-Index Plan (Arrow) + Current Implementation Status

context_summary.environment: Disaggregation: storage server processes data; host issues custom NVMe commands to storage device.
context_summary.data_path: Data does not return to host; storage/runtime processes data in-place using LBA ranges.
context_summary.host_role: Compute LBA extents and send NVMe passthrough commands (io_uring + nvme_uring_cmd).

components.name: Host (compute)
responsibilities: Resolve file path to physical extents (FIEMAP).
Convert extents to LBA segments (slba, nblocks).
Issue NVMe passthrough commands (opcode 0xD4) over io_uring.
(Optional) For Arrow: compute extent index from metadata without moving full data to host.

components.name: Storage server + storage/runtime
responsibilities: Receive NVMe passthrough requests for LBA ranges.
Process data in-place on storage side.
Avoid host data transfer in disaggregated path.

components.name: Dataset files
responsibilities: TXT: raw bytes, direct LBA read is valid.
Arrow: structured IPC with metadata/footer and column buffers.
Arrow requires mapping of text column buffer ranges to LBA extents for runtime that does not parse Arrow.

data_flow: Host reads filesystem metadata (FIEMAP) -> LBA extents.
Host sends NVMe opcode=0xD4 with {slba, nblocks}.
Storage/runtime processes data at those LBA ranges.
Result is written to storage-side output buffers/files (no host round-trip).

arrow_extent_index_plan.problem_statement: Storage/runtime should not parse Arrow; host must precompute text column LBA extents without copying full data to host.
arrow_extent_index_plan.key_point: TXT LBA extents do not exist unless a TXT file is materialized. For Arrow, we must map column buffer byte-ranges -> LBA extents.
host_side_steps: Parse Arrow IPC metadata/record batch metadata.
Locate text column buffer byte ranges (offsets/data buffers).
Translate file byte ranges -> LBA extents using FIEMAP.
Emit an extent-index manifest (for runtime to read by LBA only).

data_transfer.minimum_required: Arrow metadata + (optional) offsets buffer if row-level mapping is required.
data_transfer.note: Offsets buffers are much smaller than full text data and can be read by host without violating disaggregation goals.

runtime_requirements: No Arrow parser needed if it consumes extent-index only.
Runtime must read LBA extents and interpret them as raw text chunks (based on index).

cons: Extra pre-processing cost and storage overhead.
Complex runtime; heavier footprint on storage server.

paper_positioning.recommended: Keep Arrow original + host-generated extent index + runtime LBA-only processing.
rationale: Aligns with control-plane (host) vs data-plane (storage) separation.

compute.arrow_text_dump: Implemented as host-side Arrow -> TXT converter (CLI + pybind).

storage_runtime.text_only_pipeline: Implemented (assumes raw text in shared memory).

open_questions: Extent-index format: per-record vs per-batch granularity?
Runtime contract: how to interpret extent-index into logical text rows?
Index storage: embed inside Arrow metadata vs sidecar JSON/Binary?
next_steps: Define extent-index schema (JSON/Binary) with fields: file, column, batch_id, byte_range, lba_extents.
Implement host-side Arrow metadata parser to emit extent-index.
Update storage/runtime to consume extent-index and read LBA ranges.
